title: Business Chamber golf day helps empower entrepreneurs
description: GEORGE NEWS - The George Business Chamber...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The George Business Chamber (GBC) held a corporate golf day in support of its The Entrepreneur programme at Fancourt on 26 September.

The day was attended by 84 players making up 21 teams from businesses based in George, Cape Town, Johannesburg, Gqeberha and Pretoria.


> "The day is more than just a game," said Prof Adele Potgieter, director of the GBC, in a statement.


"It gives business leaders, entrepreneurs, and community members the opportunity to not only enjoy a round of golf, but to also contribute to a cause that promises to shape the future of entrepreneurship in our region."

She said that The Entrepreneur programme is designed to equip entrepreneurs with the skills, knowledge, and resources they need to succeed.

"It's about more than just business acumen. We focus on fostering innovation, resilience and ethical leadership.


> "The programme offers a comprehensive curriculum that includes workshops, mentorship and real-world business challenges."


Sponsors that made the day possible are: Nikki du Toit (Lekker in George), Elevate Action Centre, Europcar Van Rental, Fancourt, George Tourism, Grow Green Goal Projects, Harcourts Eden, Ilita Lodge, Kloppers, Lifta SA, Mustbuild, Old Mutual Personal Financial Advice, Outeniqua Risk Solutions, Peacehaven on Sea, Pick n Pay Minimark, Polyoak Packaging, Pro Shop George, Protea Hotel by Mariott® King George, Safari, Sanguine Spa, Savannah Helicopters, Solar Energy Africa, South Magazine, Tada Promotions, VTS Connect, Warwick Wealth, and Wilderness Hotel.

The team that placed second represented Sonqua Consulting. From left are Prof Adele Potgieter (director of the GBC), Brenda Vorster (George Airports Company manager and GBC vice-chairperson), Charl Louw (player), Mark Hartnick (player), Keith Sharpe (GBC goals projects director) and Theo Adams (player).

Prof Adele Potgieter (director of the GBC) prepares to tee off. Photo: Jacques du Toit

The media partner Group Editors also took part in the golf day. From the left are Ean Eygelaar (George South Primary School), Ds Johann Winterbach (Dutch Reformed Church George South), Sas du Plessis (Laerskool George-Suid) and Servaas de Kock (Group Editors).

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
